requests.name: TRIAL company has full write access
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: Signed in as OWNER with subscription_status=TRIAL.
Expect 201. SubscriptionGuard must allow TRIAL.

requests.name: GET /companies/me returns subscription fields (no Stripe IDs)
requests.docs:
Confirms subscription_status and trial_ends_at are returned.
NOTE: stripe_customer_id and stripe_subscription_id must NOT be in this response.

requests.name: Expire trial → run lock endpoint → company becomes LOCKED
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: SQL: UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='TRIAL', trial_ends_at='2020-01-01' WHERE id='<id>';
Expect locked > 0 on first run, 0 on second run (idempotent).

requests.name: LOCKED company can GET (read-only)
requests.docs: SubscriptionGuard only blocks write methods. GET must pass regardless of subscription_status.

requests.name: LOCKED company POST → 403 SUBSCRIPTION_LOCKED
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: company subscription_status=LOCKED.
Expect 403 with code=SUBSCRIPTION_LOCKED. Frontend apiFetch dispatches vantro:subscription-locked event.

requests.name: LOCKED company PATCH → 403 SUBSCRIPTION_LOCKED
requests.docs: PRE-REQUISITE: company subscription_status=LOCKED. Same test for DELETE.

requests.name: OWNER TRIAL → create checkout session → 201 + Stripe URL
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: OWNER, TRIAL company, STRIPE keys set.
Expect 201 + {url: 'https://checkout.stripe.com/...'}.

requests.name: OWNER LOCKED → create checkout session → 201 + Stripe URL
requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: SQL: UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='LOCKED' WHERE id='<id>';
LOCKED companies must still be able to subscribe.

requests.name: ENGINEER → create checkout session → 403
requests.docs: PRE-REQUISITE: Signed in as ENGINEER. RolesGuard must return 403.

requests.name: ACTIVE company → create checkout session → 400

tests.value: Company already has an active subscription.

requests.docs:
PRE-REQUISITE: SQL: UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='ACTIVE' WHERE id='<id>';
Expect 400 with correct message.

requests.name: ⚡ checkout.session.completed → company becomes ACTIVE
requests.docs:
TEST VIA STRIPE CLI:
  stripe listen --forward-to http://localhost:3000/api/billing/webhook
  stripe trigger checkout.session.completed

OR complete the real test checkout flow in the browser.

Verify:
  GET /api/companies/me → subscription_status = 'ACTIVE'
  payment_failed_at = null
  stripe_subscription_id is saved in DB

requests.name: ⚡ customer.subscription.deleted → company becomes LOCKED
requests.docs:
TEST VIA STRIPE CLI:
  stripe trigger customer.subscription.deleted

Verify:
  GET /api/companies/me → subscription_status = 'LOCKED'
  Write access blocked with SUBSCRIPTION_LOCKED

requests.name: ⚡ invoice.payment_failed → payment_failed_at set, status unchanged
requests.docs:
TEST VIA STRIPE CLI:
  stripe trigger invoice.payment_failed

Verify:
  GET /api/companies/me → subscription_status is UNCHANGED
  payment_failed_at is now set to a datetime
  Company is NOT locked yet

requests.name: 4-day reminder: sends once to OWNER only
requests.docs:
MANUAL TEST:

SQL to set trial ending in 4 days:
  UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='TRIAL', trial_ends_at=NOW() + INTERVAL '4 days', trial_reminder_4_days_sent_at=NULL WHERE id='<id>';

Then trigger the scheduler (at 00:15 UTC or add a test endpoint if needed).
Verify:
  - RESEND_API_KEY must be set for actual delivery
  - Only OWNER receives the email
  - ENGINEER does NOT receive the email
  - Second run: trial_reminder_4_days_sent_at is set → no second email

requests.name: 1-day reminder: sends once to OWNER only
requests.docs:
MANUAL TEST:

SQL to set trial ending in 1 day:
  UPDATE "Company" SET subscription_status='TRIAL', trial_ends_at=NOW() + INTERVAL '1 day', trial_reminder_1_day_sent_at=NULL WHERE id='<id>';

Verify:
  - RESEND_API_KEY must be set for actual delivery
  - Only OWNER receives the email, not ENGINEER
  - Second run does NOT resend (idempotent via sent_at tracking)

requests.name: ENGINEER does not receive trial reminders
requests.docs: Confirm user is ENGINEER. The TrialReminderService queries company.users WHERE role='OWNER'. ENGINEER users are not included in that query — they never receive reminder emails.
